title: Assessment Items - Elevator Failure/Interruption (Including Trapped People)

items.question: What is the first required action?
choices: Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).

items.question: Which extension/number is referenced for the initial announcement/call (if applicable)?
What is the next notification after the initial step?
choices: Inform the Safety & Security Manager through extension (7777).

If there are trapped people: record names/time/location (elevator number/code); inquire number of trapped people and health status (elderly/infants/patients); provide support/instructions and reassurance until help arrives.
Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).

items.question: Which step involves inspection/identifying the cause and estimating time to solve?
choices: Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).
Dispatch technicians to inspect, determine the cause, and prioritize releasing trapped people.
Call the Rapid Response Team (RRT) when required.

items.question: After the issue is over, what is required?
choices: Inform DCCT that the failure/interruption ended and the system is back to work.
If Civil Defense is involved, obtain a copy of the report including remarks and authority.
Announce Code Brown and inform the Planning & Preparedness Unit through extension (3432).
